message:
fix(serialization): preserve semantic trailing whitespace

bd-2vr05.5.6

- trim only terminal padding spaces in row_text helpers

- add regression coverage for trailing NBSP preservation

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.5.3","title":"task(serialization): implement HTML export with style and hyperlink fidelity","description":"Safe escaping plus deterministic style/hyperlink mapping for HTML exports.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior in this task is deterministic for fixed inputs and capability profile.
- Required unit and/or E2E tests are added for the scoped behavior.
- Scope is complete only when dependencies remain satisfied and no regressions are introduced.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This task must add or update focused unit tests for core logic touched.
- This task must link to at least one end-to-end script path that validates integrated behavior.
- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- ANSI/HTML/text export APIs are documented and deterministic.
- Golden fixtures cover representative style and unicode cases.
- Drift detection catches output regressions in CI.
- Export behavior matches declared options and defaults.

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","notes":"Execution Notes
- Serialization formats should be deterministic and suitable for diff-based regression testing.
- Prefer explicit option contracts over implicit format behavior.
- HTML export must prioritize safety and predictable structure.
- Golden tests should be shared across formats where possible to reduce fixture drift.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:45.023484701Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-03-05T22:19:00.794707202Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T22:19:00.794667998Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","frankenterm","html"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1035,"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.5.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Already implemented in crates/ftui-extras/src/export.rs via HtmlExporter. Safe HTML escaping, inline/CSS-class modes, 24-bit colors, hyperlinks, wide character support. 85 tests pass including HTML-specific tests.","created_at":"2026-03-05T22:18:59Z"}]}

+{"id":"bd-2vr05.5.6","title":"bug(serialization): preserve semantic trailing whitespace in terminal row exports","description":"Terminal row_text helpers trim with Unicode trim_end(), which removes meaningful trailing whitespace characters such as NBSP/ideographic spaces. Terminal export and snapshot text should trim only blank padding cells while preserving user-authored whitespace glyphs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T04:11:26.922691961Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T04:14:58.939826483Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T04:14:58.939460117Z","close_reason":"Fixed terminal row export trimming to preserve semantic trailing Unicode whitespace while still trimming blank padding spaces. Added regression tests in ftui-render and ftui-harness terminal models. Verification: fmt, diff check, targeted tests, terminal_model tests for both crates, workspace check, workspace clippy -D warnings, UBS.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["serialization","terminal-model","unicode"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T04:11:26.922691961Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.6","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): unicode width policy and grapheme correctness","description":"Background
- Unicode correctness is a standards and trust issue; errors here break real user text.
- Width ambiguity, grapheme clustering, and erase semantics are common sources of subtle bugs.

Goal
- Establish explicit unicode policy controls and correct runtime behavior across rendering/edit/search paths.

In Scope
- Width policy configuration and presets.
- Grapheme cluster handling for ZWJ/combining marks.
- Correct erase/backspace/cursor semantics.
- Search normalization aligned with policy settings.
- Conformance fixture corpus and CI gate.

Out of Scope
- Silent behavior changes without policy transparency.

Why This Matters
- Required for standards compliance and international usability.

Acceptance Criteria
- Unicode-heavy fixtures pass deterministically.
- Policies are explicit and reflected in behavior.
- Regressions are caught by conformance suite.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Feature behavior is validated by comprehensive unit tests that cover core invariants and edge cases.
- Feature behavior is validated by end-to-end test scripts that exercise realistic user workflows.
- E2E runs emit detailed structured logs (JSONL or equivalent) sufficient for deterministic failure triage and replay.
- CI gates for this feature fail on regression in correctness, determinism, or declared standards scope.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:35.655128351Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-18T20:07:30.516730114Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","frankenterm","standards","unicode"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.6.1","title":"task(unicode): implement width-policy configuration and presets","description":"Configurable unicode version and ambiguous-width/emoji policies with deterministic defaults.

- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Width and grapheme behavior follows selected policy presets.
- Cursor and erase semantics are correct on unicode-heavy fixtures.
- Search normalization behavior is explicit and reproducible.
- Unicode conformance corpus passes in CI.

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","notes":"Execution Notes
- Unicode behavior must be policy-driven and transparent; avoid hidden width heuristics.
- Grapheme and combining behavior should remain consistent across render, cursor, erase, and search paths.
- Add explicit fixture categories for ambiguous width, emoji ZWJ, combining marks, and normalization edge cases.
- Regression failures should indicate semantic category, not only snapshot mismatch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:46.147370796Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:23:49.892090494Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:23:49.892067922Z","close_reason":"Implemented WidthPolicy enum with Standard/CjkAmbiguousWide, threaded through TerminalEngine, added write_printable_with_width to Grid. All 537 tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","unicode","width"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
